suiteName: Configurable Products E2E - File Upload Tests

testCases.title: E2E: Create Product with Required File Upload Section and Verify Upload on Storefront
testCases.notes: Adapted: used pre-existing product 'Hoodie Base product with only File req' at /products-with-options/configurable-caps-shirts/physical-1703 instead of admin-created product. Widget visible, required File section 'only File req *' with asterisk, upload of Logo1.png (203kB) succeeded, file name shown in section header and file list with link to /api/files/... and Remove button. Price $250.00 (discounted from $300.00) unchanged after upload. Item added to cart via quantity increment, cart badge updated to 1. File reference 'Logo1.png' preserved in cart Components list. Accepted file types on this product: DOC, RTF, DOCX, TXT, PDF, XLS, XLSX, JPG, PNG, ODT. Max size 9.5MB, max 5 files.

testCases.title: E2E: Required File Section Blocks Add-to-Cart When No File Is Uploaded
testCases.notes: Without file upload: attempted add-to-cart via quantity increment. Section header changed to 'Section is required'. Toast notification appeared: 'Sorry, this product cannot be added to cart. Check your product configuration'. Cart badge remained empty -- no item added. After uploading Logo1.png: add-to-cart succeeded, cart badge showed 1, file reference 'Logo1.png' visible in cart Components list. Cleanup: removed item from cart.

testCases.title: E2E: Create Product with Optional File Section and Verify Empty and Uploaded Scenarios
testCases.notes: Used pre-existing product 'Hoodie Base with Only File non required' at /products-with-options/configurable-caps-shirts/physical. Section labeled 'Only File non required' with 'Personalize your selection further (optional)'. 'None' radio pre-selected. SCENARIO A: add-to-cart without file succeeded at $300.00. Components list in cart showed only 'Edit configuration' link (no file reference). SCENARIO B: uploaded Netflix-25.jpg (8.4kB), 'None' radio deselected, add-to-cart succeeded. Components list showed '1. Netflix-25.jpg'. Price $300.00 consistent in both scenarios. No console errors.

testCases.title: E2E: Uploaded File Preserved Through Cart and Checkout to Order History
testCases.notes: Skipped: This test requires completing a full checkout flow with payment to verify file persistence through checkout summary, order confirmation, customer order history, and admin order detail. The test was designed for a disposable admin-created product ('Test Config Custom Jersey'). Creating a real paid order on the shared QA environment with the pre-existing product would affect order history and financial data for the test user. File persistence from PDP to cart was already verified in CFG-E2E-021 and CFG-E2E-022.

testCases.title: E2E: File Upload - Invalid File Types Are Rejected
testCases.section: Configurable Products > E2E File Edge Cases
testCases.notes: Adapted: product accepts DOC, RTF, DOCX, TXT, PDF, XLS, XLSX, JPG, PNG, ODT (broader than CSV assumption of PNG/JPG only). Tested with truly invalid types. FILE A: video.mp4 (23MB) -- rejected with 'File format is not allowed' error text next to filename, 'Section is required' in section header, 'Fix file upload errors to continue' warning below upload zone. Remove button available. FILE B: download.avif (24kB) -- rejected with same 'File format is not allowed' error. After each rejection, 'Remove file' button cleared the file and returned upload area to empty state. After all rejections, uploaded valid Logo1.png -- accepted with no error, add-to-cart succeeded. One transient ApolloError on page reload (network), not related to file upload.

testCases.title: E2E: File Upload - Oversized File Rejected and Edge-Case File Sizes Handled Correctly
testCases.section: Configurable Products > E2E File Edge Cases
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Missing required test fixtures for size-specific validation. The product max size is 9.5MB (not 2MB as CSV assumes). Available test files: large_test.png (5MB) is under the limit and would be accepted. No PNG/JPG file exists that exceeds 9.5MB to isolate size-only rejection. Also missing: exactly-at-9.5MB boundary file, 0-byte .png file, and corrupt/truncated image file. The video.mp4 (23MB) tested in CFG-E2E-030 was rejected for format ('File format is not allowed'), not size -- unable to confirm size validation message independently.

testCases.title: E2E: File Upload - Special and Adversarial Filenames Handled Safely
testCases.section: Configurable Products > E2E File Edge Cases
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Missing required test fixtures. This test requires PNG/JPG files with specific adversarial filenames: (A) 'my logo.png' (spaces), (B) 'logo@2x (final).jpg' (special characters), (C) 200+ character filename, (D) '../../etc/passwd.png' (path traversal). These specially-named files do not exist in test-data/uploads/ directory. The test runner agent cannot create new files. Recommendation: add these files to test-data/uploads/ or use /qa-seed-data to generate them.

testCases.title: E2E: Upload File Then Remove It Before Add-to-Cart Re-Enforces Required File Validation
testCases.section: Configurable Products > E2E File Edge Cases
testCases.notes: Step 1: Uploaded paperstraw.JPg (112kB) -- file name and size shown, section header displayed 'paperstraw.JPg'. Step 2: Clicked Remove file button -- upload area returned to empty/default state, section header changed to 'Section is required', no file name or thumbnail visible. Step 3: Attempted add-to-cart (qty increment to 1) -- blocked with toast 'Sorry, this product cannot be added to cart. Check your product configuration'. Cart badge remained empty. Step 4: Re-uploaded newchanel.jpg (17kB) -- upload completed, section header showed 'newchanel.jpg'. Step 5: Add-to-cart succeeded -- cart badge showed 1, 'in Cart' indicator showed 1. Validation constraint correctly re-enforced after file removal and correctly cleared after re-upload.
